# Auto-wake heartbeat skips every pulse: get_unread_count uses wrong JSON path ($.type vs $.label)

**Origin Session ID:** b1839431-cba1-4b6d-913f-27b09e472e67

## Context

Discovered while diagnosing an 8h43m idle gap across all 3 swarm agents (@neo-opus-ada, @neo-gemini-pro, @neo-gpt) on 2026-05-02 → 2026-05-03 morning, despite running `swarm-heartbeat.sh` daemons launched per-identity.

## Root cause (substrate-schema mismatch — same family as #10619 Cycle 1)

`ai/scripts/swarm-heartbeat.sh:66` `get_unread_count()` queries:

```sql
WHERE json_extract(n.data, '$.type') = 'MESSAGE'
```

But MESSAGE nodes in the Memory Core graph use `$.label = 'MESSAGE'`, not `$.type`. The query returns 0 unread regardless of mailbox state.

The token-economy gate immediately below (line 161) then says:

```sh
if [ "$unread" -eq 0 ] && [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
    continue
fi
```

So **every heartbeat pulse silently skips** in the unread-only state. No pulse injection ever fires, even when the mailbox holds dozens of unread messages.

## Empirical confirmation (cross-family, 2 agents)

| Agent | `$.type` query | `$.label` query | Mailbox preview unread |
|---|---|---|---|
| @neo-opus-ada | 0 | 48 | 48-49 |
| @neo-gpt | 0 | 20 | (matches Codex inventory) |

Same SQL with the corrected JSON path returns the expected counts on both substrates.

## Fix shape

Single-line query correction:

```diff
-    local count=$(sqlite3 "$DB_PATH" "SELECT count(DISTINCT n.id) FROM Nodes n JOIN Edges e ON n.id = e.source AND e.type = 'SENT_TO' WHERE json_extract(n.data, '$.type') = 'MESSAGE' AND ...
+    local count=$(sqlite3 "$DB_PATH" "SELECT count(DISTINCT n.id) FROM Nodes n JOIN Edges e ON n.id = e.source AND e.type = 'SENT_TO' WHERE json_extract(n.data, '$.label') = 'MESSAGE' AND ...
```

Plus an inline comment referencing #10619 Cycle 1 (the AGENT_MEMORY hollow-success pattern that's structurally identical) so future RAs catch the category.

## Avoided traps

- ❌ **Don't fold this into the active-idle non-tmux delivery design lane.** That's a separate scoping concern (`tmux send-keys` only addresses tmux harnesses). This ticket is the substrate-truth fix that unblocks the existing path; the design lane is what makes the path useful for non-tmux harnesses.
- ❌ **Don't fold into identity-normalization hardening.** Independent root cause; same structural family (substrate-schema drift) but different surface. Separate small PR.
- ❌ **Don't add a `$.type` fallback "for compatibility."** No live MESSAGE rows use `$.type`. The pre-existing spec passed only because no test exercised this path with realistic data — same as the AGENT_MEMORY case in #10619.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `swarm-heartbeat.sh:66` query uses `$.label = 'MESSAGE'`
- [ ] Spec coverage: regression test that asserts `get_unread_count` returns nonzero when MESSAGE-labelled rows exist in the graph (mirrors the positive-extraction test pattern landed in #10619 Cycle 2)
- [ ] PR body cites empirical query results from both my and @neo-gpt's substrates as ground-truth anchors

## Cross-family review

@neo-gpt already proposed this work split in [MESSAGE:3d80ed90...](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues) and confirmed the bug independently — primary reviewer once PR opens.

## Provenance

- Empirical anchor: 8h43m idle gap on 2026-05-02→05-03 with 3 swarm-heartbeat instances running, ZERO pulse output despite 104 expected cycles. Logs at `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/heartbeat-*.log` showed only "Starting Sleep-Cycle MVP Heartbeat Wrapper..." and "Booting Agent Session..." — no recovery messages, no pulse output, because every cycle silently skipped at the token-economy gate.
- Pattern anchor: structurally identical to PR [#10619](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10619) Cycle 1 finding (AGENT_MEMORY query targeted `$.label='MEMORY'` + `$.properties.agent` against substrate that uses `'AGENT_MEMORY'` + `userId`). Both are "test passes but substrate path returns hollow data" — pre-existing spec exercised only the empty-DB fallback path, not the substrate query.

## Timeline

- 2026-05-03T08:44:19Z @neo-opus-ada assigned to @neo-opus-ada
- 2026-05-03T08:44:20Z @neo-opus-ada added the `bug` label
- 2026-05-03T08:44:20Z @neo-opus-ada added the `ai` label
- 2026-05-03T08:44:20Z @neo-opus-ada added the `regression` label
- 2026-05-03T08:46:46Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #10623
- 2026-05-03T08:59:21Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `22274a0` - "test(ai): add fixture-backed regression coverage for swarm-heartbeat unread count (#10622)

Per @neo-gpt's PR #10623 Cycle 1 RA: structural assertions on the
script source proved drift-resistance but did not encode the #10622
acceptance criterion ("get_unread_count returns nonzero when MESSAGE-
labelled rows exist"). Adding fixture-backed behavioral coverage.

New test cases:

1. `get_unread_count returns 1 for a fixture with one unread MESSAGE-
   labelled row` — extracts the production function definition from
   the script source, redefines it inside a bash subshell, points
   DB_PATH at a temp SQLite fixture pre-seeded with one unread MESSAGE
   row + one read MESSAGE row + a SENT_TO edge per row. Asserts the
   count is 1 (proving both the corrected `$.label` query AND the
   readAt-IS-NULL filter behave correctly).

2. `legacy $.type query returns 0 against the same fixture (regression-
   shape proof)` — runs the legacy `$.type = 'MESSAGE'` query directly
   against the same fixture via sqlite3 and asserts 0. Locks in the
   regression direction: if this ever flips to 1, either the live
   schema migrated or someone mass-rewrote MESSAGE rows, both warrant
   reviewing the test alongside the script.

Both tests share a beforeEach that creates `tmp/swarm-heartbeat-test-
<pid>-<ts>-<rand>/test-graph.sqlite` with a minimal Nodes+Edges schema
mirroring `memory-core-graph.sqlite` (foreign keys preserved; GraphLog
triggers omitted since the fixture exercises only the read path).

Tests: 4/4 pass (2 structural + 2 fixture-backed)."
- 2026-05-03T09:17:32Z @tobiu referenced in commit `f0d237b` - "fix(ai): align swarm-heartbeat unread query with MESSAGE schema (#10622) (#10623)

* fix(ai): align swarm-heartbeat unread query with MESSAGE schema (#10622)

`swarm-heartbeat.sh:get_unread_count()` queried `$.type = 'MESSAGE'` but
MESSAGE nodes use `$.label`. Returned 0 unread regardless of mailbox
state. The token-economy gate (line 161) then silently skipped every
pulse, making the heartbeat substrate a no-op for active idle agents.

Empirical anchor (cross-family confirmation, 2026-05-03):
- @neo-opus-4-7: $.type=0 vs $.label=48
- @neo-gpt: $.type=0 vs $.label=20
- 8h43m idle gap with 3 swarm-heartbeat instances running, 0 pulse
  output across 104 expected cycles per logs at
  `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/heartbeat-*.log`

Same family of substrate-schema drift as #10619 Cycle 1 (AGENT_MEMORY
hollow-success on similar JSON-path mismatch). Inline comment cites
the structural anchor so future RAs catch the category.

Adds `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/swarm-heartbeat.spec.mjs`:
- Positive: get_unread_count queries `$.label = 'MESSAGE'`
- Negative drift guard: `$.type = 'MESSAGE'` MUST NOT reappear
- Surface contract: integer-string output preserved across both
  DB-missing and DB-present branches

Tests: 2/2 pass.

* test(ai): add fixture-backed regression coverage for swarm-heartbeat unread count (#10622)

Per @neo-gpt's PR #10623 Cycle 1 RA: structural assertions on the
script source proved drift-resistance but did not encode the #10622
acceptance criterion ("get_unread_count returns nonzero when MESSAGE-
labelled rows exist"). Adding fixture-backed behavioral coverage.

New test cases:

1. `get_unread_count returns 1 for a fixture with one unread MESSAGE-
   labelled row` — extracts the production function definition from
   the script source, redefines it inside a bash subshell, points
   DB_PATH at a temp SQLite fixture pre-seeded with one unread MESSAGE
   row + one read MESSAGE row + a SENT_TO edge per row. Asserts the
   count is 1 (proving both the corrected `$.label` query AND the
   readAt-IS-NULL filter behave correctly).

2. `legacy $.type query returns 0 against the same fixture (regression-
   shape proof)` — runs the legacy `$.type = 'MESSAGE'` query directly
   against the same fixture via sqlite3 and asserts 0. Locks in the
   regression direction: if this ever flips to 1, either the live
   schema migrated or someone mass-rewrote MESSAGE rows, both warrant
   reviewing the test alongside the script.

Both tests share a beforeEach that creates `tmp/swarm-heartbeat-test-
<pid>-<ts>-<rand>/test-graph.sqlite` with a minimal Nodes+Edges schema
mirroring `memory-core-graph.sqlite` (foreign keys preserved; GraphLog
triggers omitted since the fixture exercises only the read path).

Tests: 4/4 pass (2 structural + 2 fixture-backed).
